channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI is changing the way we grieve and perceive death
item.description: Imagine attending a funeral where the person who has died speaks directly to you, answering your questions and sharing memories. This happened at the funeral of Marina Smith, a Holocaust educator who died in 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: China’s newest humanoid robot is ready to serve like never before
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a new bipedal humanoid robot that stands over 5 feet tall and can tackle a wide range of tasks in different settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech leaders' message to Biden
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adobe will kill its popular 20GB Photoshop & Lightroom plans very soon, so make sure you make the right move
item.description: Adobe phases out 20GB Creative Cloud plan, hiking the monthly costs by 50% to encourage switching to more expensive annual plans.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn will launch on Sunday—initiating Bezos’s challenge to SpaceX
item.description: Jeff Bezos‘s Blue Origin is set for an inaugural launch of its giant New Glenn rocket on Sunday, a long-awaited first leap to Earth orbit that sets up one of the biggest challenges yet to industry dominance enjoyed by Elon Musk‘s SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Farming in the Future: Tech Innovations for Sustainability
item.description: Farming in the future: tech innovations for sustainability
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness
item.description: Current and former executives say the federal technology themes for 2025 will be similar to 2024 with the addition of efficiency and effectiveness. The post For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Zuckerberg says Biden officials would 'scream,' 'curse' at Meta team over COVID takedown requests
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said Biden administration officials would “scream” and “curse” at his employees when they disagreed with the government’s takedown requests over pandemic-related content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: As Prime Video wraps up its third season doing NFL games with an AFC wild-card matchup between the Pittsburgh Steelers and Baltimore Ravens on Saturday night, the “Prime Vision” feed and use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the broadcasts continue to change the way football fans are able to analyz
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI taught me to be a (slightly) better badminton player at CES
item.description: I am not what you would call a finely tuned athletic machine. I am, if anything, an outdated lawnmower engine held together by duct tape and rust. So when I was offered the opportunity to let AI help make me a better athlete at CES, I jumped at the opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Happy New Year! We begin the new year with the top five procurement themes that will shape the federal acquisition marketplace in 2025 and beyond
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: AI will transform the enterprise ‘in a generation,’ say CDOs
item.description: Despite the many concerns around generative AI, businesses are continuing to explore the technology and put it into production, the 2025 AI and Data Leadership Executive Benchmark Survey revealed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra may quietly ditch this excellent S Pen feature
item.description: According to a new rumor, this year’s highest-end Samsung flagship may ship without a Bluetooth-enabled S Pen, which would mean losing access to a handful of great features.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Xiaomi releases six (six!) new cheap Android phones to rival the Samsung Galaxy A series
item.description: Xiaomi has released six new budget and mid-range phones under its Poco and Redmi sub-brands, with a spokesperson telling us that the brand is focused on “democratizing” flagship features.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Chinese auto giant wants to make flying cars your next commute option
item.description: An automotive maker in China has developed a flying car that is setting the stage for a new era in how we think about commuting and connectivity in cities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 10 most in-demand tech jobs for 2025
item.description: As we head into 2025, businesses are dialed in on hiring roles such as software engineer, software developer, and end-user support, as well roles centered around cloud, database management, and systems engineering and administration, according to Robert Half Technology’s 2025 IT salary report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Uncertainty over Trump’s electric vehicle policies clouds 2025 forecast for carmakers
item.description: S&P Global Mobility expects global sales of 15.1 million battery electric vehicles in 2025, which would mark a 30% jump. Battery electric vehicles are expected to make up 16.7% of the market share for light vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This fake app clone will steal everything you type on your Android
item.description: Security researchers have found a fake app pretending to be a version of the messaging app Telegram, and hackers are using the app to spread malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Despite potential TikTok ban, Bay Area parents to host digital safety events at their high schools
item.description: The social media platform TikTok partnered with the National PTA to launch an online safety awareness campaign at high schools throughout the country, which includes three high schools in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Nollyanne Delacruz, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: In a collision between free speech and national security, the Supreme Court will hear arguments over the fate of TikTok, a digital age cultural phenomenon that roughly half the U.S. population uses for entertainment and information.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta makes major move back to free speech and ends 3rd-party fact-checking program
item.description: Meta recently announced it will cease working with third-party fact-checking organizations to flag misleading posts on Instagram, Facebook and Threads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How AI will shape work in 2025
item.description: AlphaSense is a market intelligence platform that uses generative artificial intelligence (genAI) and natural language processing to help organizations find and analyze insights from sources like financial reports, news, earnings calls, and proprietary documents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The ‘Worst in Show’ CES products put your data at risk and cause waste, privacy advocates say
item.description: So much of the technology showcased at CES includes gadgets made to improve consumers’ lives — whether by leveraging AI to make devices that help people become more efficient, by creating companions to cure loneliness or by providing tools that help people with mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI detects ovarian cancer better than human doctors: Study
item.description: AI based models achieved an accuracy rate of 86% for detecting ovarian cancer, compared to 82% for human experts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World
